title: Day Tracker: Add summary stats panel (all-time, week, today hours & minutes)

requirements.summary: Add a prominent statistics summary panel at the top of the DayTracker page displaying four metrics: (1) All Days Hours — total sum of all logged hours across every date, (2) This Week Hours — sum of logged hours for the current calendar week (Monday to Sunday), (3) Today Hours — sum of logged hours for today, and (4) Today Minutes — today's total hours converted to minutes. The panel must react immediately to entry changes and use date-fns for date calculations.
acceptanceCriteria: A stats panel is visible on the Day Tracker page with four cards: 'All Days Hours', 'This Week Hours', 'Today Hours', and 'Today Minutes'.
'All Days Hours' shows the correct sum of all entry hours across all dates in the user's namespaced localStorage store.
'This Week Hours' shows the correct sum of hours for entries with a date falling in the current Mon–Sun week.
'Today Hours' shows the correct sum of hours for entries dated today.
'Today Minutes' shows today's total hours converted to total minutes (e.g., 1.5 hours = 90 minutes).
All four values update immediately (without page reload) when an entry is added, edited, completed, or deleted.
The stats panel is styled consistently with the existing teal/slate-green theme and is fully responsive.
If there are no entries, all four values display as 0.

file_operations.description: Add a statistics summary panel component above the existing entry list that displays four metric cards: 'All Days Hours', 'This Week Hours', 'Today Hours', and 'Today Minutes'. Compute 'All Days Hours' by summing all entry hours from the entries array. Compute 'This Week Hours' by filtering entries where the date falls within the current Mon–Sun week using date-fns (startOfWeek/endOfWeek with weekStartsOn: 1) and summing their hours. Compute 'Today Hours' by filtering entries matching today's ISO date and summing their hours. Compute 'Today Minutes' by multiplying 'Today Hours' by 60. Render each metric in a Card component (from shadcn/ui) with a clear label and large numeric value. Style the panel with a responsive grid layout (1 column on mobile, 2–4 columns on larger screens) and use the existing teal/slate-green theme colors. Ensure all calculations update reactively when entries change (the entries array is already reactive via React Query). Position the stats panel immediately above the week navigation controls or entry list section.
